pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - Protecting waterbodies in India  - Video

pageCaption.value: Determined from an early age to revive and protect the waterbodies near his home in Chennai, 2012 Rolex Awards for Enterprise Laureate Arun Krishnamurthy gave up a promising career at Google to devote his life to rejuvenating the lakes in his native India and beyond.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex Awards Video - Reviving India’s blighted lakes

metadescription.value: In 2021, Arun Krishnamurthy united 10,000 volunteers in his mission to restore and protect dying waterbodies in India. Watch the video on rolex.org

text.value: Determined from an early age to revive and protect the waterbodies near his home in Chennai, 2012 Rolex Awards for Enterprise Laureate Arun Krishnamurthy gave up a promising career at Google to devote his life to rejuvenating the lakes in his native India and beyond. Since launching the Environmentalist Foundation of India (EFI) in 2007, he has mobilized thousands of people, including scores of young volunteers, to help clean, restore and rehabilitate some 164 of India’s lakes that have long been subject to pollution and the impacts of climate change. Krishnamurthy credits the Award with boosting his resolve to help safeguard the environment, as manifest in Rolex’s Perpetual Planet initiative.

pageCaption.value: Brazilian environmentalist José Márcio Ayres, who died in March 2003, devoted his life to the challenge of combining protection of the Amazon forest with improving the living conditions of its native people.

metadescription.value: Brazilian environmentalist José Márcio Ayres, who died in March 2003, devoted his life to the challenge of combining protection of the Amazon forest with improving the living conditions of its native people.

pageCaption.value: American Christine Keung is using her college education as a force for good by tackling rural pollution in northwest China, where her father was sent during the Cultural Revolution. Her ambition is to help authorities find long-term solutions to hazardous waste, and women – who bear the disproportionate cost of environmental degradation as men migrate to cities for work – are the key to her project.

metadescription.value: American Christine Keung is using her college education as a force for good by tackling rural pollution in northwest China, where her father was sent during the Cultural Revolution. Her ambition is to help authorities find long-term solutions to hazardous waste, and women – who bear the disproportionate cost of environmental degradation as men migrate to cities for work – are the key to her project.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards Video - A vanished forest reborn

pageCaption.value: Working with dedicated farming families, Laury Cullen Jr. is restoring Brazil’s Atlantic Forest while contributing to the local economy and helping to fight climate change.

metadescription.value: Laury Cullen Jr.’s Dream Map initiative has overseen the planting of four million trees in Brazil’s Atlantic Forest. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - Preventing car accidents in India - Video

pageCaption.value: Motivated by the unnecessary death of his cousin in a road accident where bystanders typically ignored the victim’s plight, successful Indian businessman and 2010 Rolex Awards for Enterprise Laureate Piyush Tewari gave up his career to concentrate on helping to save the lives of his compatriots on the country’s chaotic roads.

WaPageTitle.value: Saving lives on India’s roads - Video

metadescription.value: Since winning a Rolex Award, Piyush Tewari has expanded solutions for fatality-free roadways to 15 states across India. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Saving lives on India’s roads - Video

pageTitle.value: The call of the cloud forest - Rolex Awards Video

pageCaption.value: Restoring the dwindling biodiversity of the unique montane cloud forests of Sri Lanka through the Agrapatana Montane Forest Restoration Initiative earned conservationist Rohan Pethiyagoda a Rolex Award for Enterprise in 2000.

WaPageTitle.value: The call of the cloud forest - Video

metadescription.value: Since the Rolex Award in 2000, Rohan Pethiyagoda has stepped up his initiative to restore Sri Lanka's unique ecosystem. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: The call of the cloud forest - Video

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards Video - How Greenland’s ice impacts climate change

pageCaption.value: For British glacial microbiologist Joseph Cook, the blooming of a rainforest of microscopic life in the rapidly warming Arctic holds not only mystery and beauty – but also menace. The colours reflect both the unanticipated changes wrought by human activity on the planet and the hazards of accelerating sea level rise, drowning the world’s coastal cities.

metadescription.value: 2016 Rolex Young Laureate Joseph Cook is carrying out research on microscopic life in the melting of the planet’s ice sheets. Watch the new video on rolex.org.
